heading: HOV facilities
label: 166 HOV facilities

children.heading: .—Authority of public authorities
children.label: 1 .—Authority of public authorities
children.text: A public authority that has jurisdiction over the operation of a HOV facility shall establish the occupancy requirements of vehicles operating on the facility.

children.heading: .—Occupancy requirement
children.label: 2 .—Occupancy requirement
children.text: Except as otherwise provided by this section, no fewer than two occupants per vehicle may be required for use of a HOV facility.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Notwithstanding the occupancy requirement of subsection (a)(2), the exceptions in paragraphs (2) through (5) shall apply with respect to a public authority operating a HOV facility.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the public authority shall allow motorcycles and bicycles to use the HOV facility.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: A public authority may restrict use of the HOV facility by motorcycles or bicycles (or both) if the authority certifies to the Secretary that such use would create a safety hazard and the Secretary accepts the certification.

children.heading: .—Acceptance of certification
children.label: ii .—Acceptance of certification
children.text: The Secretary may accept a certification under this subparagraph only after the Secretary publishes notice of the certification in the Federal Register and provides an opportunity for public comment.

children.label: A
children.text: establishes requirements for clearly identifying the vehicles;

children.label: B
children.text: establishes procedures for enforcing the restrictions on the use of the facility by the vehicles; and

children.label: C
children.text: provides equal access under the same rates, terms, and conditions for all public transportation vehicles and over-the-road buses serving the public.

children.label: A
children.text: establishes a program that addresses how motorists can enroll and participate in the toll program;

children.label: B
children.text: develops, manages, and maintains a system that will automatically collect the toll; and

children.label: i
children.text: manage the demand to use the facility by varying the toll amount that is charged;

children.label: ii
children.text: enforce violations of use of the facility; and

children.label: iii
children.text: ensure that over-the-road buses serving the public are provided access to the facility under the same rates, terms, and conditions as public transportation buses.

children.label: i
children.text: alternative fuel vehicles; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any motor vehicle described in section 30D(d)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.

children.heading: .—Other low emission and energy-efficient vehicles
children.label: B .—Other low emission and energy-efficient vehicles
i
children.text: establishes a program that addresses the selection of vehicles under this paragraph; and

children.label: ii
children.text: establishes procedures for enforcing the restrictions on the use of the facility by the vehicles.

children.heading: .—Amount of tolls
children.label: C .—Amount of tolls
children.text: Under this paragraph, a public authority may charge no toll or may charge a toll that is less than or equal to tolls charged under paragraph (4).

children.heading: .—Blood transport vehicles
children.label: 6 .—Blood transport vehicles
children.text: The public authority may allow blood transport vehicles that are transporting blood between a collection point and a hospital or storage center to use the HOV facility if the public authority establishes requirements for clearly identifying such vehicles.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Notwithstanding section 301, tolls may be charged under paragraphs (4) and (5) of subsection (b), subject to the requirements of section 129.

children.heading: .—Toll revenue
children.label: 2 .—Toll revenue
children.text: Toll revenue collected under this section is subject to the requirements of section 129(a)(3).

children.heading: HOVFacility Management, Operation, Monitoring, and Enforcement.—
children.label: d HOVFacility Management, Operation, Monitoring, and Enforcement.—
A
children.text: Establishing, managing, and supporting a performance monitoring, evaluation, and reporting program for the facility that provides for continuous monitoring, assessment, and reporting on the impacts that the vehicles may have on the operation of the facility and adjacent highways and submitting to the Secretary annual reports of those impacts.

children.label: B
children.text: Establishing, managing, and supporting an enforcement program that ensures that the facility is being operated in accordance with the requirements of this section.

children.label: C
children.text: Limiting or discontinuing the use of the facility by the vehicles whenever the operation of the facility is degraded.

children.label: I
children.text: increasing the occupancy requirement for HOV lanes;

children.label: II
children.text: varying the toll charged to vehicles allowed under subsection (b) to reduce demand;

children.label: III
children.text: discontinuing allowing non-HOV vehicles to use HOV lanes under subsection (b); or

children.label: IV
children.text: increasing the available capacity of the HOV facility.

children.heading: .—Notice of approval or disapproval
children.label: ii .—Notice of approval or disapproval
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date of receipt of a plan under clause (i), the Secretary shall provide to the public authority a written notice indicating whether the Secretary has approved or disapproved the plan based on a determination of whether the implementation of the plan will make significant progress toward bringing the HOV facility into compliance with the minimum average operating speed performance standard.

children.label: I
children.text: the actions taken to bring the HOV facility into compliance; and

children.label: II
children.text: the progress made by those actions.

children.heading: .—Compliance
children.label: E .—Compliance
children.text: If the public authority fails to bring a facility into compliance under subparagraph (D), the Secretary shall subject the public authority to appropriate program sanctions under section 1.36 of title 23, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations), until the performance is no longer degraded.

children.label: I
children.text: the waiver is in the best interest of the traveling public;

children.label: II
children.text: the public authority is meeting the conditions under subparagraph (D); and

children.label: III
children.text: the public authority has made a good faith effort to improve the performance of the facility.

children.heading: .—Condition
children.label: ii .—Condition
children.text: The Secretary may require, as a condition of providing a waiver under this subparagraph, that a public authority take additional actions, as determined by the Secretary, to maximize the operating speed performance of the facility, even if such performance is below the level set under paragraph (2).

children.heading: .—Definition of minimum average operating speed
children.label: A .—Definition of minimum average operating speed
i
children.text: 45 miles per hour, in the case of a HOV facility with a speed limit of 50 miles per hour or greater; and

children.label: ii
children.text: not more than 10 miles per hour below the speed limit, in the case of a HOV facility with a speed limit of less than 50 miles per hour.

children.heading: .—Standard for determining degraded facility
children.label: B .—Standard for determining degraded facility
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the operation of a HOV facility shall be considered to be degraded if vehicles operating on the facility are failing to maintain a minimum average operating speed 90 percent of the time over a consecutive 180-day period during morning or evening weekday peak hour periods (or both).

children.heading: .—Management of low emission and energy-efficient vehicles
children.label: C .—Management of low emission and energy-efficient vehicles
children.text: In managing the use of HOV lanes by low emission and energy-efficient vehicles that do not meet applicable occupancy requirements, a public authority may increase the percentages described in subsection (f)(3)(B)(i).

children.heading: .—Certification of Low Emission and Energy-Efficient Vehicles
children.label: e .—Certification of Low Emission and Energy-Efficient Vehicles

children.text: issue a final rule establishing requirements for certification of vehicles as low emission and energy-efficient vehicles for purposes of this section and requirements for the labeling of the vehicles; and
establish guidelines and procedures for making the vehicle comparisons and performance calculations described in subsection (f)(3)(B), in accordance with .section 32908(b) of title 49

children.label: A
children.text: methanol, denatured ethanol, or other alcohols;

children.label: B
children.text: a mixture containing at least 85 percent of methanol, denatured ethanol, and other alcohols by volume with gasoline or other fuels;

children.label: C
children.text: natural gas;

children.label: D
children.text: liquefied petroleum gas;

children.label: E
children.text: hydrogen;

children.label: F
children.text: coal derived liquid fuels;

children.label: G
children.text: fuels (except alcohol) derived from biological materials;

children.label: H
children.text: electricity (including electricity from solar energy); or

children.label: I
children.text: any other fuel that the Secretary prescribes by regulation that is not substantially petroleum and that would yield substantial energy security and environmental benefits, including fuels regulated under , Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations).section 490 of title 10

children.heading: HOV .—facility
children.label: 2 HOV .—facility
children.text: The term “HOV facility” means a high occupancy vehicle facility.

children.label: A
children.text: has been certified by the Administrator as meeting the Tier II emission level established in regulations prescribed by the Administrator under section 202(i) of the Clean Air Act () for that make and model year vehicle; and42 U.S.C. 7521(i)

children.label: i
children.text: is certified by the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency, in consultation with the manufacturer, to have achieved not less than a 50-percent increase in city fuel economy or not less than a 25-percent increase in combined city-highway fuel economy (or such greater percentage of city or city-highway fuel economy as may be determined by a State under subsection (d)(2)(C)) relative to a comparable vehicle that is an internal combustion gasoline fueled vehicle (other than a vehicle that has propulsion energy from onboard hybrid sources); or

children.label: ii
children.text: is an alternative fuel vehicle.

children.heading: .—Over-the-road bus
children.label: 4 .—Over-the-road bus
children.text: The term “over-the-road bus” has the meaning given the term in section 301 of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 ().42 U.S.C. 12181

children.heading: .—Public authority
children.label: 5 .—Public authority
children.text: The term “public authority” as used with respect to a HOV facility, means a State, interstate compact of States, public entity designated by a State, or local government having jurisdiction over the operation of the facility.

children.label: A
children.text: provides designated public transportation (as defined in section 221 of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 () or provides public school transportation (to and from public or private primary, secondary, or tertiary schools); and42 U.S.C. 12141

children.label: i
children.text: is owned or operated by a public entity;

children.label: ii
children.text: is operated under a contract with a public entity; or

children.label: iii
children.text: is operated pursuant to a license by the Secretary or a public authority to provide motorbus or school vehicle transportation services to the public.

children.heading: .—Consultation of MPO
children.label: g .—Consultation of MPO
children.text: If a HOV facility charging tolls under paragraph (4) or (5) of subsection (b) is on the Interstate System and located in a metropolitan planning area established in accordance with section 134, the public authority shall consult with the metropolitan planning organization for the area concerning the placement and amount of tolls on the facility.
